mechanism_narrative: A low or falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when policy stays near the zero lower bound and does not re-tighten.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or if the sign of the effect turns negative in a rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a recession-warning threshold signals weakening household income and credit quality, so equity prices fall over the next 20 trading days as investors price lower earnings and higher default risk.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes with UNRATE above 9.5% do not show negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if more than 60% of such episodes are positive.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized contraction in PAYEMS and RSAFS signals broad demand weakening in payroll income and retail spending, which should depress near-term corporate revenue expectations and push S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines while RSAFS also declines on a 3-month change basis
failure_condition: Rejected if joint declines in PAYEMS and RSAFS do not outperform either series alone in predicting negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not negative in at least 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in INDPRO indicates industrial output is contracting faster than normal, which implies weaker real activity and lower expected earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 1.0% month over month
failure_condition: Rejected if month-over-month INDPRO drops larger than 1.0% are followed by non-negative average 20-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior collapse marks a housing-led recovery in demand and construction activity, supporting cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 3 consecutive months after a 6-month decline
failure_condition: Rejected if housing rebounds of this form do not lead to positive average 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal’s 60-day hit rate is indistinguishable from unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is rising while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, real rates fall and financial conditions ease, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation > FEDFUNDS by at least 2 percentage points
failure_condition: Rejected if the inflation-minus-policy-rate spread does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if positive outcomes are not more frequent than baseline in spread-positive periods.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series confirms job losses are broadening, and equities should continue to discount weaker consumer spending over the next 5 trading days, producing negative returns.
failure_condition: Rejected if monthly PAYEMS declines are not followed by negative average 5-day SPX returns, or if the directional accuracy remains below 50% for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals that real output and household spending are both recovering, which should lift revenue expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive while RSAFS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Rejected if joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS conditions fail to produce above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal underperforms either proxy alone.
